residual_risks.risk: normalizeFilename() on filenames without clear separators (e.g., 'lesson1final.mp4' → 'Lesson1Final') produces no-space output that is still more readable than a UUID but may not parse well visually. The plan acknowledges this.

Search index in toSearchableLesson now indexes normalizeFilename(video.filename) instead of the raw slug. Users searching by the original filename slug (e.g., 'demo-pdf-2') will no longer get exact-title matches — only fuzzy (0.2) or partial matches against the normalized form. Acceptable trade-off per plan

testing_gaps.gap: Test file tests normalizeFilename('') twice (one test named 'falsy input' but does not pass undefined/null). Since the function type is string, TS prevents passing undefined/null from app code, but the 'falsy input' test name is misleading and doesn't exercise the runtime guard branch against non-string falsy values.

No unit test for the youtubeVideoId fallback chain in the call sites. Plan states 'covered by unifiedSearch tests' but those tests are not included in this diff. The fallback chain normalizeFilename(fn) || youtubeVideoId || id is only exercised by the existing palette/snapshot tests.
